title: ‘WandaVision’: What Worked, What Didn’t, and What’s Next With Mallory Rubin
format: Sean and Mallory dig through the 'WandaVision' finale, then Sean reviews 'Raya and the Last Dragon' and talks to Kevin Macdonald about 'The Mauritanian.'

segments.heading: ‘WandaVision’ Season One Autopsy
segments.note: the finale, Wanda's arc, and where phase four goes next

films.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron
films.note: Wanda and Pietro's real MCU introduction. Neither host's favorite entry, but they call it foundational to everything that follows.

films.title: Captain America: Civil War
films.note: The twins first appeared in its post-credits stinger before Age of Ultron proper.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Vision's death at Wanda's own hands. Mallory calls it one of her all-time favorites.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The two watched it together in theaters and were, in Sean's words, vibrating.

films.title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
films.note: Its post-blip In Memoriam sequence and its timeline (set after WandaVision, oddly) both come up.

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: Backstory for Monica's mother Maria, and the Nick Fury/Talos body-double question raised by the finale's stinger.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: Was originally slated to kick off phase four before the pandemic pushed WandaVision into that slot instead.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: The show's real target: Agatha's Darkhold, the nexus talk, and Wanda's villain-or-hero status all point here.

films.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
films.note: Named as one of the films WandaVision is quietly setting up.

films.title: The Devil's Advocate
films.note: The internet's short-lived theory that Al Pacino's devil would resurface as Mephisto.

films.title: The Evil Dead
films.note: Cited for director Sam Raimi's history with the Necronomicon, ahead of him bringing the Darkhold to Doctor Strange 2.

films.title: Raya and the Last Dragon
films.note: Sean went in with low expectations after Disney's muted marketing and came out impressed by the cast, the action, and Awkwafina's dragon.

films.title: The Mauritanian
films.note: Macdonald on adapting Mohamedou Ould Slahi's smuggled Guantanamo memoir, and on Jodie Foster cutting her own lines down to the bone.

films.title: Touching the Void
films.note: Raised as an earlier example of Macdonald's pull toward undiscovered, off-the-map settings.

films.title: The Last King of Scotland
films.note: Same thread: Macdonald's recurring interest in unfamiliar places and people.

films.title: Dear Comrades!
films.note: Macdonald's pick for the last great thing he'd seen: Andrei Konchalovsky's black-and-white Soviet strike drama, made at 86.

films.title: Runaway Train
films.note: Mentioned as Konchalovsky's earlier, very mainstream Jon Voight vehicle.

films.title: Tango & Cash
films.note: Another Konchalovsky Hollywood credit, brought up in the same breath.

TV: The Falcon and the Winter Soldier

Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madn.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
